name: prototype-to-production
description: Convert design prototypes (HTML, CSS, Figma exports) into production-ready components. Analyzes prototype structure, extracts design tokens, identifies reusable patterns, and generates typed React components. Adapts to existing project tech stack with React + TypeScript as default.
capabilities: Analyzes HTML prototypes to identify component boundaries and structure
Extracts design tokens (colors, typography, spacing) from CSS styles
Detects project tech stack (React, Vue, Svelte, Angular) and TypeScript support
Generates typed React components with forwardRef pattern and accessibility
Applies atomic design methodology to decompose complex prototypes
Converts inline styles to Tailwind CSS or styled-component patterns
author: ArieGoldkin
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill with no executable code. Static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting React/TypeScript syntax (className, onClick) as shell commands and cryptographic patterns. No scripts, network calls, or credential access exist in this skill.
valueStatement: Transforming design prototypes into production code is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill provides a systematic workflow to analyze prototypes, extract design tokens, and generate type-safe React components that match your existing project conventions.
limitations: Does not execute code or modify files directly - provides guidance only
Does not connect to external APIs or fetch remote resources
Does not handle backend logic or database integration

useCases.title: Productionize prototypes
useCases.description: Convert HTML/CSS mockups into maintainable React components with proper typing

useCases.title: Handoff to code
useCases.description: Prepare Figma exports with clear component structure for developer implementation

useCases.title: Rapid component creation
useCases.description: Quickly build production UI from super-design iterations without manual conversion

promptTemplates.title: Basic conversion
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the prototype-to-production skill to convert this HTML prototype to a React TypeScript component. Extract design tokens and apply atomic design principles.

promptTemplates.title: Figma export
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply the prototype-to-production skill to convert this Figma-exported HTML into production-ready React components. Include accessibility attributes.

promptTemplates.title: Design tokens
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the prototype-to-production skill to extract design tokens from the prototype CSS. Create a design-tokens.json file with colors, typography, and spacing.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-component
promptTemplates.prompt: Decompose this complex prototype into atomic components using the prototype-to-production workflow. Generate component-base.tsx and component-with-variants.tsx templates.

bestPractices: Always extract repeated patterns (2+ occurrences) into reusable components
Include accessibility attributes (aria-*, keyboard support) from the start
Extract design tokens before component generation for consistency
Match the generated code to your project naming conventions (PascalCase for components)
antiPatterns: Converting a clickable div instead of using semantic button elements
Skipping design token extraction leading to hardcoded values across components
Not considering responsive breakpoints from the prototype
Ignoring accessibility requirements in favor of visual-only implementation

faq.question: Which frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: React (default), Vue, Svelte, and Angular. Detects framework from package.json and generates appropriate component format.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Figma exports?
faq.answer: Yes. It detects Figma-specific class names (figma- prefix) and converts exported HTML to React components.

faq.question: How are design tokens extracted?
faq.answer: The skill analyzes inline styles and CSS classes to identify colors, typography, spacing, and border radius patterns.

faq.question: Is my code sent to external servers?
faq.answer: No. This is a prompt-based skill with no network access. All processing happens within your AI context.

faq.question: Can I use this with Tailwind CSS?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports converting inline styles to Tailwind classes when Tailwind is detected in the project.

faq.question: How does this compare to manual conversion?
faq.answer: Provides systematic workflow, extracts consistent design tokens, and generates properly typed components following best practices.
